title: Asyncio Task Groups, Backpressure, Queues, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A university documentation platform owner. They need RFC 9309 and sitemap handling, conservative URL identity, graph and frontier evidence, trap prevention, per-origin spacing, retry control, structured concurrency, cancellation, and a reliable stop contact. Planned artifact: Deliver Polite Bounded Site Crawler with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Define conservative scheme, host, port, path, percent-encoding, query-order, and tracking-parameter normalization without merging distinct resources.
Reserve an admitted URL atomically before scheduling and reconcile queued, active, completed, redirected, rejected, and retry states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Robots Group Matching
steps.instruction:
project case — A project site-quality team handles case 8537 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate TaskGroup ownership, bounded queues, semaphore order, cancellation, cleanup, and stable result evidence; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Cancellation Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-cancellation-cleanup-gf0o7x" that uses case 2362 to validate TaskGroup ownership, bounded queues, semaphore order, cancellation, cleanup, and stable result evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Cancellation Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Cancellation Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Cancellation Cleanup, keep Cancellation Cleanup connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-cancellation-cleanup-gf0o7x
# Competency: scraper-cancellation-cleanup.
# Case 2362: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_cancellation_cleanup_gf0o7x(scheduled_tasks: int = 32, completed_tasks: int = 32, queue_peak: int = 12, queue_limit: int = 16, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_gf0o7x = "gf0o7x-x7o0fg-2362"
    assert evidence_variant_gf0o7x
    evidence_axes_gf0o7x = (
        "authorizationgf0o7x",
        "url2362",
        "responsex7o0fg",
        "treegf0o7x2362",
        "frontier2362gf0o7x",
        "reportx7o0fg2362",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_gf0o7x)) == 6
    if scheduled_tasks < 0 or completed_tasks < 0:
        return False, completed_tasks, "task-count"
    if completed_tasks != scheduled_tasks:
        return False, completed_tasks, "task-reconciliation"
    if queue_peak < 0 or queue_peak > queue_limit:
        return False, queue_peak, "queue-backpressure"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_tasks == scheduled_tasks and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_tasks, "structured-crawl-concurrency"
